english: hold, to gain a livelihood; kyiḿ-gyi so-tsis (khyim gyi so tshis) household, housekeeping, farming; kyiḿ-gyi ríg-pa (khyim gyi rig pa) knowledge, experience in housekeeping and farming; kyiḿ-med-pa (khyim med pa) homeless, without a home; therefore esp. as opp. to the life of a homeless and unmarried priest: kyím-gyi byá-ba (khyim gyi bya ba) or las (las), 1. domestic business, 2. lay-life, worldly life; cf. also many of the compounds. kyím-la óṅ-ba (khyim la 'ong ba), ytóṅ-ba (gtong ba) to get married, to be given in marriage, respecting the female part Glr., Mil. — 2. the signs of the Zodiac, which is called kyiḿ-gyi _kór-lo (khyim gyi 'khor lo), viz.. lug (lug) ram, ylaṅ (glang) bull, _kríg-pa ('khrig pa) (pairing) twins, kárkata (Ssk.) crab, séṅ-ge (seng ge) lion, bú-mo (bu mo) virgin, sraṅ (srang) balance, sdig(-pa) (sdig pa) scorpion, yžu (gzhu) (bow) archer, ču-srin (chu srin) (sea-monster) capricorn, búm-pa (bum pa) water-bearer, nya (nya) fishes. To these 12 signs however the corresponding Tibetan figures are not ༡ to ༡༢, but ༠ to ༡༡, as seems to be the usage in astronomical science. There is moreover a division into 27 'lunar mansions' much in use; v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar). — 3. double-hour, the time of two hours; or the twelfth part of the time of the apparent daily rotation of the heavens and consequently also of the zodiac, or, as we should say, the time of the passing of a sign of the zodiac through the meridian. — 4 Cs.: halo, or circle round the sun or moon. — 5. Symbolic numeral: 12. Comp. and deriv. kyiḿ-táb(s) (khyim thab(s)) husband, frq.; also wife; kyiḿ-táb-la ytóṅ-ba (khyim thab la gtong ba) to give in marriage, to give away a woman for a wife; kyiḿ-táb-mo (khyim thab mo) wife, housewife, Cs. — kyiḿ-bdág (khyim bdag) master of the house, husband; owner of a house, citizen; kyiḿ-bdág-ma (khyim bdag ma) fem. — kyiḿ-pa (khyim pa) 1. layman, 2. Cs.: surrounded by a halo (kyiḿ 4) (khyim 4); kyiḿ-pai _pyógs-su (khyim pa'i phyogs su) sbyín-pa (sbyin pa) given away to laymen Dzl.; kyiḿ-par _dug (khyim par 'dug) or gnas (gnas) he lives as a layman; _pyis kyiḿ-pai tsúl-ċan-gyi rnál-_byor-pa (phyis khyim pa'i tshul can gyi rnal 'byor pa) a devout man, who lives outwardly like a layman Mil. — kyiḿ-pa-pa (khyim pa pa) a houseowner, peasant, farmer, husband; kyiḿ-pa-ma (khyim pa ma) housewife. — kyiḿ bya (khyim bya) domestic fowl, cock, hen, poultry W., C. — kyiḿ-mi (khyim mi) family of, a house, household Cs. — kyiḿ-tsáṅ (khyim tshang) id. — kyiḿ-tsér (khyim mtsher) Glr. 51, 10, usually kyiḿ-mtsés (khyim mtshes), kyiḿ-mtsés-pa (khyim mtshes pa), fem. kyiḿ-mtsés-ma (khyim mtshes ma) neighbour. — kyiḿ-žág (khyim zhag), kyiḿ-zlá (khyim zla), kyiḿ-ló (khyim lo) 'a zodiacal day, month, year' (?) Cs. — kyiḿ-sa (khyim sa) earth, dust, dirt (in a house), sweepings W., *kyiḿ-sa dú-ċe (khyim sa du ce), spúṅ-ċe (spung ce) to sweep (a floor), to sweep together.
Sch.: whale (if at all correct, it must be taken as mythological signification, no Tibetan having ever known of the existence of real whales).

Cs., kyú-mo (khyu mo) Pth. flock, herd, lúg-kyu (lug khyu) a flock of sheep, mdzó-mo-kyu (mdzo mo khyu) a herd of bastard cows, ynág-kyu (gnag khyu) of horned cattle; kyu skóṅ-ba (khyu skong ba) to keep, tend a flock or herd; company, band, gang, troop, mi-kyu (mi khyu) Cs. a company of men, bú-mo-kyu (bu mo khyu) a bevy of girls, dmag-kyú (dmag khyu) a troop of soldiers; kyú-nas _búd-pa (khyu nas 'bud pa) to exclude from the company Pth., C.; kyu-sná _drén-pa (khyu sna 'dren pa) to go before, to take the lead of a troop, a flock Mil.; kyu-mčóg (khyu mchog) bell-wether; also the most distinguished amongst a number of men, the first, chief, head Pth., kyu-mčóg-ma (khyu mchog ma) fem.; kyu tságs-pa (khyu tshags pa) vb.n., Sch. to collect, to gather in flocks.
(Sch. also kyúṅ-mo (khyung mo)) the Garuda bird, a mythical bird, chief of the feathered race. kyúṅ-šog-ċan (khyung shog can) = pyag-rdór (phyag rdor).
a small round basket of reed Cs.; kyuṅ-ríl (khyung ril) is said to be in C. a large cylindrical basket, the same as kun-dúm (kun dum) Ld., v. rkón-pa (rkon pa).
('Garuda claw') Med., (s.: n. of a medicinal root, pseudo-zedoary; kyuṅ-rgód (khyung rgod) Med. id (?).
